title: Marbella Private Yacht Charter at Sunset: Costs, Boats and Tips for 2026
topic: private yacht charter sunset in Marbella
html:
Planning a private yacht charter at sunset in Marbella offers a memorable way to experience the Costa del Sol, with departures from Puerto José Banús and prices typically ranging from €1,500 for a small motorboat half-day to €5,000+ for a fully crewed luxury yacht. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators to find the perfect vessel for your evening afloat.

How much does it cost to rent a private yacht for sunset in Marbella?

Prices for a private sunset charter in Marbella start from around €1,500 for a half-day trip and rise to €5,000 or more depending on the vessel and services. The final cost depends on boat size, crew, catering, and season. You can compare options and book a charter online through BoatHire24 to see real-time availability and pricing.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most sunset charters depart from Puerto José Banús (Puerto Banús), the famous marina in the heart of Marbella’s luxury scene. Other real departure points include Puerto Deportivo de Marbella (the main marina), Cabopino Marina just east of Marbella, and the nearby port of Estepona to the west. For a shorter sail, the Bay of Marbella offers calm waters, while heading south towards the Barbate coast or the Straits of Gibraltar provides a longer horizon view.

Which boat to rent for a sunset cruise

  1. Motor yacht (Azimut), best for speed and luxury, ideal for couples or small groups up to 8 guests, from €2,000 for a half-day.
  2. Sailing catamaran (Lagoon), best for stability and space, great for families or groups of up to 12 guests, from €1,800 for a half-day.
  3. Express cruiser (Fairline), best for a sporty feel with open deck, suitable for up to 6 guests, from €1,500 for a half-day.
  4. Luxury flybridge (Sunseeker), best for premium comfort and panoramic views, accommodates up to 10 guests, from €3,500 for a half-day.

For a specific example, the Azimut 58 for hire offers a spacious flybridge and is a popular choice for sunset cruises along the Marbella coast.

Sunset charter costs in Marbella: what to expect

  • Shared sunset tour: from €80 per person, typically 2, 3 hours, including drinks.
  • Small motorboat half-day: from €1,500 for a private charter of up to 6 guests, 4 hours.
  • Catamaran full-day: from €2,500 for up to 12 guests, 6 hours with crew.
  • Luxury crewed yacht: from €5,000 for up to 10 guests, 4, 6 hours with full catering.

To understand how to book and what to expect, consult the booking FAQ for details on deposit, cancellation, and what’s included.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

In Spain, a boating licence is required for bareboat charters, but crewed charters include a skipper so no licence is needed by the guests. For bareboat hire, skipper qualifications vary by boat power; the operator will confirm exact requirements. The high season runs from June to September, when the Poniente wind often creates a light chop in the Bay of Marbella. A local tip:

For the best sunset views, book a charter that departs between 18:30 and 19:30 from Puerto Banús, heading west towards the Sierra Blanca mountains for a dramatic sun-drenched backdrop.

At a glance

  • Price band: €1,500, €5,000+ for private charter
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 3, 4 hours typical for sunset
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests on most private vessels
  • Currency: EUR
  • Departure points: Puerto Banús, Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, Cabopino, Estepona

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I bring my own food and drink on a sunset charter?

Many operators allow you to bring your own provisions, but some offer catering packages; always check with the owner when booking.

What is the cancellation policy for a private yacht charter?

Most providers require a deposit and have a 48-hour free cancellation window, but exact terms vary by listing on BoatHire24.

Are sunset charters suitable for children?

Yes, most boats welcome children, and a skipper ensures safety; confirm guest counts when booking to ensure life jackets are available.

How far offshore do sunset cruises go?

Typically cruises stay within a few nautical miles of the coast, often passing by the Marbella beaches and Cabopino dunes.
